The zygomatic bone, also known as the cheekbone, is part of the facial skeleton that forms much of the lateral orbital wall. It articulates directly or indirectly with several bones including the frontal (temporal fossa), maxilla (zygomaticomaxillary buttress), nasal bone (inferior surface). However, it does NOT articulate with the temporal bone which lies more medially behind the external auditory meatus and is separated from this by other structures such as the tympanic plate of the sphenoid bone.

It articulates directly or indirectly with several bones including the frontal (temporal fossa), maxilla (zygomaticomaxillary buttress), nasal bone (inferior surface). However, it does NOT articulate with the temporal bone which lies more medially behind the external auditory meatus and is separated from this by other structures such as the tympanic plate of the sphenoid bone.
